fields.label: Prometheus server URL
fields.description: URL of your Prometheus server. If Prometheus is running locally, use http://localhost:9090.

fields.label: Authentication method
fields.description: Choose an authentication method to access the data source

fields.label: User
fields.description: The username of the data source account

fields.label: Password
fields.description: The password of the data source account

fields.label: Add self-signed certificate
fields.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

fields.label: CA Certificate
fields.description: Your self-signed certificate

fields.label: TLS Client Authentication
fields.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

fields.label: ServerName
fields.description: A Servername is used to verify the hostname on the returned certificate

fields.label: Client Certificate
fields.description: The client certificate can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

fields.label: Client Key
fields.description: The client key can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

ui.placeholder: Begins with --- RSA PRIVATE KEY CERTIFICATE ---

fields.label: Skip TLS certificate validation
fields.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

fields.label: Allowed cookies
fields.description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.

fields.label: Timeout
fields.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds

fields.label: Custom HTTP Headers
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request. Header names are stored in jsonData (httpHeaderName<N>); header values are write-only in secureJsonData (httpHeaderValue<N>).

validations.message: Header name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens.

fields.label: Manage alerts via Alerting UI
fields.description: Manage alert rules for this data source. To manage other alerting resources, add an Alertmanager data source.

fields.label: Allow as recording rules target
fields.description: Allow this data source to be selected as a target for writing recording rules.

fields.label: Scrape interval
fields.description: This interval is how frequently Prometheus scrapes targets. Set this to the typical scrape and evaluation interval configured in your Prometheus config file. If you set this to a greater value than your Prometheus config file interval, Grafana will evaluate the data according to this interval and you will see less data points. Defaults to 15s.

fields.label: Query timeout
fields.description: Set the Prometheus query timeout.

fields.label: Default editor
fields.description: Set default editor option for all users of this data source.

fields.label: Disable metrics lookup
fields.description: Checking this option will disable the metrics chooser and metric/label support in the query field's autocomplete. This helps if you have performance issues with bigger Prometheus instances.

fields.label: Prometheus type
fields.description: Set this to the type of your prometheus database, e.g. Prometheus, Cortex, Mimir or Thanos. Changing this field will save your current settings. Certain types of Prometheus supports or does not support various APIs. For example, some types support regex matching for label queries to improve performance. Some types have an API for metadata. If you set this incorrectly you may experience odd behavior when querying metrics and labels. Please check your Prometheus documentation to ensure you enter the correct type.

fields.label: Version
fields.description: Use this to set the version of your Prometheus instance if it is not automatically configured. The option list depends on the selected Prometheus type (see PromFlavorVersions.ts in @grafana/prometheus).

fields.label: Cache level
fields.description: Sets the browser caching level for editor queries. Higher cache settings are recommended for high cardinality data sources.

fields.label: Incremental querying (beta)
fields.description: This feature will change the default behavior of relative queries to always request fresh data from the prometheus instance, instead query results will be cached, and only new records are requested. Turn this on to decrease database and network load.

fields.label: Query overlap window
fields.description: Set a duration like 10m or 120s or 0s. Default of 10m. This duration will be added to the duration of each incremental request.

fields.label: Disable recording rules (beta)
fields.description: This feature will disable recording rules. Turn this on to improve dashboard performance

fields.label: Custom query parameters
fields.description: Add custom parameters to the Prometheus query URL. For example timeout, partial_response, dedup, or max_source_resolution. Multiple parameters should be concatenated together with '&'.

fields.label: HTTP method
fields.description: You can use either POST or GET HTTP method to query your Prometheus data source. POST is the recommended method as it allows bigger queries. Change this to GET if you have a Prometheus version older than 2.1 or if POST requests are restricted in your network.

fields.label: Series limit
fields.description: The limit applies to all resources (metrics, labels, and values) for both endpoints (series and labels). Leave the field empty to use the default limit (40000). Set to 0 to disable the limit and fetch everything — this may cause performance issues. Default limit is 40000.

fields.label: Use series endpoint
fields.description: Checking this option will favor the series endpoint with match[] parameter over the label values endpoint with match[] parameter. While the label values endpoint is considered more performant, some users may prefer the series because it has a POST method while the label values endpoint only has a GET method.

fields.label: Exemplars
fields.description: Exemplar trace ID destinations. For each configured destination, the plugin renders a link on exemplar labels — either to an internal Grafana tracing data source (datasourceUid) or an external URL. The label whose value carries the trace ID is 'name' (defaults to 'traceID' when new entries are added).

fields.label: URL
fields.description: The URL of the trace backend the user would go to see its trace

fields.label: URL Label
fields.description: Use to override the button label on the exemplar traceID field.

fields.label: Data source
fields.description: The tracing data source the exemplar link should navigate to. Setting this makes the exemplar an internal link and takes precedence over url.

When set, Grafana appends this value to Prometheus query requests as the max_samples_processed_warning_threshold URL parameter. Not exposed in the Prometheus config editor (the PromSettings component only renders this input when its showQuerySamplesProcessedThresholdFields prop is true — the plugin never passes it), but the field is parsed by the backend (pkg/promlib/models/settings.go:41).

When set, Grafana appends this value to Prometheus query requests as the max_samples_processed_error_threshold URL parameter. Not exposed in the Prometheus config editor (feature-flagged off — see maxSamplesProcessedWarningThreshold).

relationships.description: Basic auth credentials. Both fields are used together when root.basicAuth is true.
TLS mutual auth: enabling jsonData.tlsAuth requires serverName + client cert + client key. See @grafana/plugin-ui TLSClientAuth.tsx.
Self-signed / custom CA verification: enabling jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert requires the CA certificate PEM in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.
Overlap window only takes effect when incrementalQuerying is enabled (PromSettings.tsx:429).

instructions.msg: Prometheus authentication is built on Grafana's HTTP settings model, not a discriminator field. The editor's three visible auth methods write these flags: 'Basic authentication' sets root.basicAuth=true and provides root.basicAuthUser + secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword; 'Forward OAuth Identity' sets jsonData.oauthPassThru=true; 'No Authentication' leaves both false. Only one of basicAuth / oauthPassThru should be true at a time — the editor's onAuthMethodSelect (HttpSettings.tsx:59-69) always writes both. The upstream utility convertLegacyAuthProps (@grafana/plugin-ui) also recognises root.withCredentials for 'cross-site access control', but this plugin does not offer it in its visibleMethods.

Minimal Basic-auth payload: {"url":"http://localhost:9090","basicAuth":true,"basicAuthUser":"<user>","jsonData":{},"secureJsonData":{"basicAuthPassword":"<password>"}}. Minimal No-auth payload: {"url":"http://localhost:9090","jsonData":{}}. Minimal OAuth-forward payload: {"url":"http://localhost:9090","jsonData":{"oauthPassThru":true}}. root.url is required — the backend admission handler (pkg/promlib/admission_handler.go:51) rejects a missing URL. Trailing slashes are safe: the client uses path.Join to build /api/v1/... under the base URL.

TLS is independent of the auth method. jsonData.tlsAuth=true enables mTLS and requires jsonData.serverName plus secureJsonData.tlsClientCert and secureJsonData.tlsClientKey. jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true enables server-cert verification against a custom CA and requires secureJsonData.tlsCACert. Both can be enabled together. jsonData.tlsSkipVerify=true bypasses verification entirely and should only be used for testing. All TLS fields are consumed by the SDK's settings.HTTPClientOptions(ctx) call in pkg/promlib/client/transport.go:18.

secureJsonData values (basicAuthPassword, tlsCACert, tlsClientCert, tlsClientKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.basicAuthPassword == true) instead of secureJsonData.

jsonData.httpMethod defaults to POST (see getOptionsWithDefaults, PromSettings.tsx:74-82 and pkg/promlib/models/settings.go:82-87 ApplyDefaults) and must be POST or GET; the backend's Validate rejects any other value. jsonData.timeInterval (Scrape interval, default '15s' in the tooltip only) and jsonData.queryTimeout (default '60s' in the placeholder only) are duration strings validated by the DURATION_REGEX (constants.ts:11) — empty means 'use Grafana defaults'. If neither is set, no default is written to storage; the backend treats empty QueryTimeout as 'no explicit timeout parameter'.

Editor-hidden but backend-parsed knobs: jsonData.maxSamplesProcessedWarningThreshold and jsonData.maxSamplesProcessedErrorThreshold are read by ParsePromOptions (pkg/promlib/models/settings.go:41-42) but the PromSettings component only shows their inputs when showQuerySamplesProcessedThresholdFields=true, and the Prometheus plugin's own ConfigEditor never passes that prop (ConfigEditor.tsx:47). Provisioning can still set them via jsonData. Also note jsonData.oauthPassThru, jsonData.manageAlerts, and jsonData.allowAsRecordingRulesTarget. Custom HTTP headers ARE now modeled by the jsonData_httpHeaders array field using an indexedPair storage mapping: each header's name is written to jsonData.httpHeaderName<N> and its value is written write-only to secureJsonData.httpHeaderValue<N> (starting at N=1), exactly as @grafana/plugin-ui's CustomHeaders component and the SDK HTTPClientOptions expect. The per-header httpHeaderValue<N> secrets are dynamic and are therefore not listed among the static SecureJsonDataKeys.
